observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to complete the physical inventory taking, render a report thereon and update the Property Records based on the result of the inventory and reconcile with the accounting records.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s Annual Audit Report (AAR).

observations.recommendation: 2. Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of property and accounting records must be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s Annual Audit Report (AAR).

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor as well as the Municipal Treasurer agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to liquidate her cash advances as soon as possible considering that the amounts have already been distributed to the beneficiaries;
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR under paragraph 5.0 part II.

observations.recommendation: 4. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare promptly the adjusting journal entries as soon as the Municipal Treasurer submits the Fund Utilization Report (FUR).
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, agreed to return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agencies after confirming that the purpose of the fund transfer agreed in the MOA has already been completed.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR under paragraph 5.0 part II.

observations.recommendation: 8. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to request for the write-off on the balances of fund transfers recorded in the Due to LGUs account under the General and Trust Funds that are more than 10 years following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR under paragraph 3.0 part II.

observations.recommendation: 9. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to regularly monitor the receipts and utilization of fund transfers and return any unspent amount to the Source Agencies after the implementation depending on the condition in the MOA.
observations.reason: Regular monitoring is made but the ‘Due to’ balances in the books could not be removed because of the difficulty in the retrieval of documents from long time ago.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to (a) develop an effective tax collection campaign to enhance the collection of RPT and SET Receivables; (b) prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies and post the said list at the Municipality and/or publish notice of delinquency; (c) issue notice of delinquency and/or final demand letter to concerned property owner and avail remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Included in this year’s AAR under paragraph 6.0 part II.

observations.recommendation: 15. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that no additional cash advance be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: 17. Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the adjustment of stale checks in the books as at year-end.
observations.reason: Stale checks totaling ₱70,751.03 were made.

observations.recommendation: 18. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to update records on the additions and disposals of all inventory accounts for the reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Included in this year’s AAR under paragraph 3.0 part 2.

observations.recommendation: 19. We also recommended and the Inventory Custodian and Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct regular reconciliation of inventory accounts to ascertain its existence and correctness.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of inventory accounts not yet conducted.

observations.recommendation: 20. Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to properly write off inventory account which is proven that is no longer existing, and or already sold/used, and prepare the necessary journal entries to fairly presents the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Inventory count still on-going.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: a.) Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with the necessary information and data in the columns provided therein for evaluation, and b.) Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the steps and procedures enumerated in Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to generate additional income out of the proceeds from sale thereof.

observations.recommendation: 23. observations.recommendation: 24. The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the necessary fund utilization reports and return fund balances of all completed projects to the NGAs concerned pursuant to the prescribed regulations.

observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to restate the value of the Land to its revalued amounts, and
recognize any revaluation increases and/or decreases in the books once the
appraisal/revaluation is done; and

1.16 Henceforth, ensure proper recording of transactions to their appropriate
accounts.

1.17 Furthermore, we recommended and the Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Assessor to coordinate and exert
collaborative efforts in reconciling the market value of Land as required under
existing rules and regulations.

2.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Inventory
Committee to complete the physical inventory of PPE, render a report thereon
and update the property records based on the results of the inventory, so
reconciliation with accounting records will be made; and

2.8 Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of property and
accounting records be done regularly and regarded with utmost importance.

3.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify and review
the existence of all dormant accounts, prepare the necessary schedules, and
initiate proper disposition procedures as stated in Section 7.0 of COA Circular
No. 2023-008; and

3.11 Monitor regularly the reconciliation of accounts and settlement of any
unliquidated cash advances to prevent the accumulation of dormant and
undocumented accounts in the ensuing years.

4.15 We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
4.15.1 Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the IAR accurately
reflects the actual delivery dates and quantities, and that the RIS aligns
with the actual fuel issuance;
4.15.2 Direct the BAC to adhere to GPPB resolutions and regulations, ensuring
that fuel purchases are based on prevailing market prices rather than a
fixed price;
4.15.3 Direct the Municipal Accountant to verify that payments for FOL are
aligned with the retail pump price in compliance with existing guidelines;
4.15.4 Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the DTT and FCR to the
Office of the Auditor for proper review. Henceforth, ensure that these are
attached to the disbursement vouchers upon submission;
4.15.5 Assign an officer in each office to monitor the preparation and submission
of DTT and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and
4.15.6 Henceforth, strengthen internal controls for fuel management to prevent
inconsistencies, improve transparency, and ensure compliance with
procurement and financial regulations.

5.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to call the attention of the
employees who are still in service, to fully settle the amount advanced as soon
as possible. Otherwise, withhold their salaries until the cash advances are fully
settled; and

5.10 Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that that
no additional cash advance be granted to any official or employee with pending
unliquidated cash advance.

6.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to conduct an extensive
information campaign to raise taxpayer awareness and promote participation
in the tax amnesty program under Section 30 of RA No. 12001, to assist the
municipality in reaching their collection target in subsequent years.

7.9 We recommended and the MDRRM Officer agreed to collaborate with other
implementing offices, to review and prioritize DRRM PPAs under the
LDRRMF ensuring its completion within the year; and

7.10 Strengthen coordination during the planning and budgeting stages to avoid
duplication of activities already covered by other funding sources.

8.4 We recommended and the LSB agreed to strictly comply with the submission
requirements of quarterly SEF utilization reports to all required agencies, as
prescribed under existing laws and regulations, and to prevent future lapses in
transparency and accountability.

5. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to submit a plan on how these obligations will be settled without the corresponding cash back-up or how these obligations will be met.
observations.reason: Audit of cash and cash analyses for CY 2024 revealed that cash balance is sufficient for its outstanding current obligations and trust liabilities.

observations.recommendation: 6. Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate and observe the limitations in the disbursement of government funds pursuant to Section 337 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that no cash overdraft in any government fund will be incurred at any given time of the year.
observations.reason: Audit of cash and cash analyses for CY 2024 revealed that cash balance is sufficient for its outstanding current obligations and trust liabilities.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development Office, the Municipal Engineer’s Office and other offices that are involved in the implementation of 20% Development Fund projects, agreed to immediately address the causes of delays and fast track the implementation of the unimplemented projects. Regular monitoring of project implementation should be assigned to specific office to ensure implementation and the achievement of the desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to the constituents.

11. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the MDRRM Officer and other Implementing Offices to implement and ensure that the programs, projects, and activities funded by the LDRRMF are executed in a timely manner and closely monitor the implementation to strengthen the capacity of the municipality to respond in case disaster may occur anytime.

13. We recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to periodically update and submit an updated appraisal and assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer on time so that updating of their records be made.
observations.reason: Municipal Treasurer has a copy of the Land records.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommended that the Management direct the MDRRM Officer and other Implementing Offices to implement and guarantee that the programs, projects, and activities funded by the LDRRMF are executed in a timely manner and closely monitor the implementation to strengthen the capacity of the municipality to respond in case disaster may occur anytime.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to complete the physical inventory taking, render a report thereon and update the Property Records based on the result of the inventory and reconcile with the accounting records.
Henceforth, periodic inventory taking and reconciliation of property and accounting records must be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to submit a plan on how to settle the various obligations without the corresponding cash back-up or how these obligations will be met.
Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate and observe the limitations in the disbursement of government funds pursuant to Section 337 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that no cash overdraft in any government fund will be incurred at any given time of the year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, agreed to return the unspent balances of fund transfers to the Source Agencies after confirming that the purpose of the fund transfer agreed in the MOA has already been completed.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to request for the write-off on the balances of fund transfers recorded in the Due to LGUs account under the General and Trust Funds that are more than 10 years following the guidelines and procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to regularly monitor the receipts and utilization of fund transfers and return any unspent amount to the Source Agencies after the implementation depending on the condition in the MOA.

We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development Office, the Municipal Engineer’s Office and other offices that are involved in the implementation of 20% Development Fund projects, agreed to immediately address the causes of delays and fast track the implementation of the unimplemented projects. Regular monitoring of project implementation should be assigned to specific office to ensure implementation and the achievement of the desirable outcomes from the delivery of socio-economic development and environmental services to the constituents.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to revert the unutilized balances of the appropriations classified as MOOE to the general fund so that funds thereof may be used as supplemental budget and can be appropriated for other projects of the Municipality.
We also recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Development Coordinator agreed to ensure that balances of appropriations for MOOE are reverted to the general fund at the end of fiscal year and such shall not be made continuing in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the MDRRM Officer and other Implementing Offices to implement and ensure that the programs, projects, and activities funded by the LDRRMF are executed in a timely manner and closely monitor the implementation to strengthen the capacity of the municipality to respond in case disaster may occur anytime.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the personnel in charge in the submission of the cash advance vouchers for CF, with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents, to submit the same to the COA ATL within seven days after release of check, pursuant to Item 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to (a) develop an effective tax collection campaign to enhance the collection of RPT and SET Receivables; (b) prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies and post the said list at the Municipality and/or publish notice of delinquency; (c) issue notice of delinquency and/or final demand letter to concerned property owner and avail remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under RA No. 7160.
We recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to periodically update the land valuation and submit an updated appraisal and assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer on time so that updating of their records be made.

We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of procuring meals through cash advance and strictly follow the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. In cases where public bidding is not feasible, employ alternative modes of procurement, provided, that the conditions outlined in the Revised IRR are strictly followed.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require the MHI Head to submit immediately the required supporting documents to substantiate the validity of the SRA claims. 
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure strict compliance with the prescribed guidelines in the submission of liquidation documents for the payment of SRA.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor as head of the Municipal Development Council (MDC) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office agreed to ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20 percent Development are well planned, procurement and implementation ready with itemized appropriation for specific development projects.    
We also recommended and the Municipality agreed to discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in lump-sum amount, instead resort to line budgeting to ensure regularity and transparency of government transactions. In case lump-sum appropriations cannot be avoided, Management agreed to secure prior authority from the SB before disbursement of funds is authorized.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to provide a copy of the Program of Work on the Rehabilitation and Repair of Farm to Market Roads indicating therein that the oil is a component item in the project and to submit the oil consumption report from the Motor Pool to support the validity of expense to be charged against 20 percent DF.
The Municipal Development Council agreed to ensure that only development projects and programs included in the AIP and APP be charged against the 20 percent DF and make certain of the Municipality’s compliance with Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 in the utilization of the Development Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to intensify the real property tax collection to meet the targets so that the Municipality can provide additional funds to finance infrastructure and basic services to its constituents; and 
We also recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to periodically update the land valuation and submit an updated appraisal and assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for setting the RPT target in the beginning of each year.
We recommend for an immediate titling or the annotation of the total land purchased by the municipality from the mother title of the Lot 890-B be made.
observations.reason: Already with annotation. For submission to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor  agreed  to  undertake  complete
physical inventory to include all Property, Plant,  and  Equipment  accounts
through creation of Inventory  Committee,  and  submit  to  the  Auditor  an
updated inventory report not later than January 31 of each year.


    We also recommended and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  that  upon
receipt of the updated inventory reports, effect appropriate adjustments  to
PPE accounts at year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed  to  send  a  written
notice to the payees for the cancellation of the checks and require them  to
surrender the checks for issuance of a new one. She also  agreed  to  cancel
the checks that are still in her possession.


    Furthermore, we recommended and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to  record  the  adjustment  of  stale
checks in the books as at year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to refrain from  pre-
signing the checks so that the possibility  of  unauthorized  use/encashment
of checks be prevented.


    We also recommended that checks be signed only after  the  disbursement
vouchers are duly approved and with complete supporting documents.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to stop the  practice
of utilizing Job  Order  employees  to  collect  revenues  accruing  to  the
government.


    Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to  hire  or
assign additional regular employees to the Municipal Treasurer’s  Office  to
address the problem on the lack of manpower.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to update records on
the  additions  and  disposals   of   all   inventory   accounts   for   the
reconciliation of records.


    We  also  recommended  and  the  Inventory  Custodian   and   Municipal
Accountant agreed to conduct regular reconciliation  of  inventory  accounts
to ascertain its existence and correctness.


    Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to  properly
write off inventory account which is proven that is no longer existing,  and
or already sold/used, and prepare the necessary journal  entries  to  fairly
presents the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the LVOC Nurse Coordinator  agreed  to  furnish  the
Municipal Accountant with the detailed list of donated vaccines and in  kind
received by the Municipality.


    Furthermore, we recommended and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to
recognize in the  books  all  the  donations  received  in  accordance  with
existing regulations. In  case  of  donations  received  without  cost,  the
Municipal Accountant shall recognize the  donations  at  their  fair  market
value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained  by  reference  to
an active market or by appraisal as provided in paragraph 97  of  the  IPSAS
NO. 23.

We  recommended  and  the  Municipality  agreed   to   strengthen   its
information  dissemination  and  COVID-19  vaccination  campaign  to   fight
vaccine hesitancy and achieve the herd immunity set by the DOH.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed  to  recognize  this
financial assistance as Trust Liability under General Fund  as  mandated  by
the guidelines set by the DBM.


    We also recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to properly
appropriate the fund through  legislation  and  submit  Supplemental  Annual
Investment Plan in compliance with existing regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Planning and Development  Coordinator,
Municipal Engineer and other officials involved  in  the  implementation  of
20% Development Fund projects  agreed  to  coordinate  with  each  other  to
expedite the completion of the unimplemented projects.


    We  also  recommended  and  the  Municipal  Planning  and   Development
Coordinator, in coordination with the other officials  involved,  agreed  to
conduct regular monitoring of the status of  the  projects  to  ensure  that
these development projects are feasible and can be  implemented  within  its
schedule.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: a.) Prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with the necessary information and data in the columns provided therein for evaluation, and b.) Dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with the steps and procedures enumerated in Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 to generate additional income out of the proceeds from sale thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipality of Moises Padilla promptly remit the taxes withheld from the employees to the BIR and the monthly premium contributions and loan repayments to the GSIS amounting to ₱285,580.37 and ₱253,766.10, respectively as prescribed under Section 2.81 of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 and Section 6 of Republic Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.
observations.managementAction: LGU has given assurance to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Outstanding amount in Due to BIR will only be remitted upon total purchase of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the preparation and submission of the Monthly Report of Official Travel for each vehicle to the office of the Auditor. The report shall be certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer. Likewise, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption shall be prepared and submitted to the office of the Auditor in order to inhibit disallowance and in compliance with the above cited provisions of law.
observations.managementAction: LGU has assigned the Municipal Engineer to prepare/facilitate compliance with this finding.
observations.reason: Required reports were prepared but were not kept on file due to paper volume.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Municipality of Moises Padilla review and update its Ten Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the current and succeeding years in compliance with Section 16 of RA 9003. The Municipality must also strengthen its existing Barangay SWM Committees and fast track the creation of such for barangays with newly elected officials. Likewise, the implementation of SWM policies must be religiously enforced through various options such as the integration of SWM orientation upon the renewal/application of mayor’s permits and business licenses.
observations.managementAction: Management Plan was implemented using an old template.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management and the local school board refrain from charging non-related expenses to the Special Education Fund and henceforth comply with the above-stated regulation in prioritizing expenses.
observations.managementAction: Transactions after finding had been discussed were aligned to the regulation.
observations.reason: There are still expenses not included in prioritization of utilization of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for an immediate titling or the annotation of the total land purchased by the municipality from the mother title of the Lot 890-B be made.
observations.managementAction: As per information gathered, the original TCT had been annotated.
observations.reason: No TCT was issued in the name of the Municipality for portion already paid.
